items.name: SNMP walk Operating Table
items.description: Scanning `JUNIPER-MIB::jnxOperatingTable` for CPU, Memory, Temperature, and Fans.

items.name: Hardware model name
items.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The name, model, or detailed description of the device, indicating which product it represents, for example, `M40`.

items.name: Hardware serial number
items.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The serial number of this subject, blank if unknown or unavailable.

triggers.expression: last(/.Juniper Generic by SNMP/juniper.system.hw.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/.Juniper Generic by SNMP/juniper.system.hw.serialnumber,#2) and length(last(/.Juniper Generic by SNMP/juniper.system.hw.serialnumber))>0
triggers.name: Juniper: Device has been replaced
triggers.event_name: Device has been replaced (new serial number received)
triggers.description: Device serial number has changed. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

triggers.expression: last(/.Juniper Generic by SNMP/juniper.system.sw.os[sysDescr.0],#1)<>last(/.Juniper Generic by SNMP/juniper.system.sw.os[sysDescr.0],#2) and length(last(/.Juniper Generic by SNMP/juniper.system.sw.os[sysDescr.0]))>0
triggers.name: Juniper: Operating system description has changed
triggers.description: Operating system description has changed. Possible reasons - system has been updated or replaced. Acknowledge to close the problem manually.

discovery_rules.name: CPU and Memory discovery
discovery_rules.description:
Scanning `JUNIPER-MIB::jnxOperatingTable` for CPU and Memory.
http://kb.juniper.net/InfoCenter/index?page=content&id=KB17526&actp=search. Filter limits results to Routing Engines.

item_prototypes.name: System [{#SNMPVALUE}]: CPU utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The CPU utilization, in percent, of this subject. Zero if unavailable or inapplicable.
Reference: http://kb.juniper.net/library/CUSTOMERSERVICE/GLOBAL_JTAC/BK26199/SRX%20SNMP%20Monitoring%20Guide_v1.1.pdf

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: System [{#SNMPVALUE}]: High CPU utilization
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Juniper MX: System [{#SNMPVALUE}]: High CPU utilization (over {$JUNIPER.CPU.UTIL.CRIT}% for 5m)
trigger_prototypes.description: The CPU utilization is too high. The system might be slow to respond.

item_prototypes.name: System [{#SNMPVALUE}]: Memory utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The buffer pool utilization, in percent, of this subject. Zero if unavailable or inapplicable.
Reference: http://kb.juniper.net/library/CUSTOMERSERVICE/GLOBAL_JTAC/BK26199/SRX%20SNMP%20Monitoring%20Guide_v1.1.pdf

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: System [{#SNMPVALUE}]: High memory utilization
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Juniper MX: System [{#SNMPVALUE}]: High memory utilization (>{$JUNIPER.MEMORY.UTIL.MAX}% for 5m)
trigger_prototypes.description: The system is running out of free memory.

item_prototypes.name: Sensor [{#SNMPVALUE}]: Fan status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
Current status of the Fan tray.

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: Sensor [{#SNMPVALUE}]: Fan is in critical state

item_prototypes.name: Sensor [{#SNMPVALUE}]: Power supply status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
If are using DC power supplies, there is a known issue on PR 1064039 where the fans do not detect the temperature correctly and fail to cool the power supply causing shutdown to occur.
This is fixed in Junos 13.3R7 https://forums.juniper.net/t5/Routing/PEM-0-not-OK-MX104/m-p/289644#M14122

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: Sensor [{#SNMPVALUE}]: Power supply is in critical state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the power supply unit for errors.

item_prototypes.name: Redundancy [{#SNMPVALUE}][{#CHASSISDESCR}]: Current running state
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The current running state for the `Redundancy [{#SNMPVALUE}][{#CHASSISDESCR}]` subject.

item_prototypes.name: Redundancy [{#SNMPVALUE}][{#CHASSISDESCR}]: Reason of the last switchover
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The reason of the last switchover for the `Redundancy [{#SNMPVALUE}][{#CHASSISDESCR}]` subject.

item_prototypes.name: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: JUNIPER-MIB
The temperature in Celsius of [{#SENSOR_INFO}].

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is above critical threshold
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Juniper MX: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is above critical threshold: >{$JUNIPER.TEMP_CRIT:"Juniper MX: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as the temperature sensor status if available.

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is above warning threshold
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Juniper MX: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is above warning threshold: >{$JUNIPER.TEMP_WARN:"Juniper MX: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as the temperature sensor status if available.

dependencies.name: Juniper: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is above critical threshold

trigger_prototypes.name: Juniper: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is too low
trigger_prototypes.event_name: Juniper MX: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]: Temperature is too low: <{$JUNIPER.TEMP_CRIT_LOW:"Juniper MX: Sensor [{#SENSOR_INFO}]"}
trigger_prototypes.description: This trigger uses temperature sensor values as well as the temperature sensor status if available.

macros.description: ICMP checks, SNMP availability, device Health
Trigger evaluation period for health items
Warning threshold of ICMP packet loss in %.
Warning threshold of the average ICMP response time in seconds.
Threshold of CPU utilization for trigger in %. Can be used with interface name as context.
Threshold of status sensor for trigger. All statuses defined in valuemap `JUNIPER-MIB::jnxOperatingState`.
Threshold of memory utilization for trigger in %. Can be used with interface name as context.
Threshold of status sensor for trigger. All statuses defined in valuemap `JUNIPER-MIB::jnxOperatingState`.
Threshold of temperature sensor for trigger. Can be used with interface name as context.
Threshold of temperature sensor for trigger. Used for Routing Engine.
Threshold of temperature sensor for trigger. Can be used with interface name as context.
Threshold of temperature sensor for trigger. Can be used with interface name as context.
Threshold of temperature sensor for trigger. Used for Routing Engine.
Time interval for the SNMP availability trigger.
System name, contact, location, object ID
